questions.description: Carmen was out at a nightclub when she saw Justin, the boyfriend of her best friend Jessica.  She was about to go over a…
questions.text: Carmen was out at a nightclub when she saw Justin, the boyfriend of her best friend Jessica.  She was about to go over and say hello and ask where Jessica was when she suddenly saw Justin embrace a blonde haired woman.  Carmen was shocked and decided not to go over to Justin but instead to discreetly follow him for the night and see what he and the mystery woman got up to. As the night wore on, Carmen felt like Justin and the woman were getting more intimate with each other.  Horrified that he might be cheating on her friend, Carmen began taking pictures with her phone of the two of them.  Sure enough, as the nightclub was about to close she caught both of them in a passionate kiss and took one last picture. Carmen immediately rang Jessica and told her that she had just seen Justin at a nightclub with a blonde haired woman.  Jessica was sleepy but before Carmen could finish, she said “Oh that’s Justin’s sister, they’re very close.  They share everything, even me.”  With that the line went dead. Carmen was sick to her stomach. This was too disgusting. She decided that she would forward the picture of Justin and his sister kissing with the following caption “Justin kissing his sister, and Jessica knows and participates!”  She circulated it to everyone on her contact list, including Jessica’s mother, although she had forgotten that Jessica’s mother was in her contacts list. Jessica is horrified. The woman in question was not Justin’s sister but an ex-girlfriend and what Jessica had intended to say was “...They share everything, even meeting up at nightclubs.”  Her boyfriend was cheating on her and everyone now thinks she is a pervert. Advise Carmen as to any potential liability in tort.

questions.description: Critically analyse the legal principles applicable in determining an award of damages in a tort action.
questions.text: Critically analyse the legal principles applicable in determining an award of damages in a tort action.

questions.description: Bill is a retired person who lives in a large house on a lightly populated country road.  In the past, only people livin…
questions.text: Bill is a retired person who lives in a large house on a lightly populated country road.  In the past, only people living along this road would use it, however as a result of the new toll road, many people now use it as a method of avoiding payment of the toll by exiting the motorway, driving up Bill’s road and then rejoining the motorway on the other side of the toll plaza.  At the beginning of Autumn, Bill noticed that there was a leak from his main water connection that resulted in water running onto the road.  He meant to fix it before winter set in but had not gotten around to it. Last week, Sue was driving home very late at night.  It was a dry night but temperatures had fallen to -5C, unusually cold for that time of year.  Sue decided to avoid the toll and began to drive on Bills’ road.  Soon she found herself driving behind Mary, who lived along the road.  As Mary approached Bill’s house, she knew that the water would likely have frozen on the road and so slowed down from 50kph to 30kph.  Sue was exasperated, so she swung past Mary and accelerated sharply.  Just then Rusty the dog ran across the road having got loose from Laura’s house.  Sue braked sharply and found herself in an uncontrollable skid. As Sue skidded all over the road, she impacted with Mary’s car and as a result was hurled onto the other side of the road where some County Council workers had left a trailer parked contrary to approved procedures.  Sue is a paraplegic as a result of this sequence of events. Critically discuss the cause of the injuries suffered by Sue in any potential tort action.

questions.description: Martin had severe stomach cramps following an operation where his appendix was removed.  He went back to his doctor seve…
questions.text: Martin had severe stomach cramps following an operation where his appendix was removed.  He went back to his doctor several times who said that this was to be expected and that he should stop worrying.  After the third visit he prescribed a set of painkillers and some antibiotics figuring this would clear up any infection.  A couple of weeks later and nothing had improved.  The doctor sent Martin back to the hospital. At the hospital, Martin was examined by a junior doctor who told him that it was probably a psychosomatic issue and that the pain represented the feeling of loss for the removed appendix.  Martin asked for an X-Ray but was told that this was not necessary.  Martin went home but the next day collapsed and was taken by ambulance to the hospital.  In the emergency room, the doctor on call sent Martin for an X-Ray and at that point it became clear that there was a foreign object lodged in his body. In emergency surgery, they removed the needle of a syringe.  Unfortunately because of the delay, the needle had caused an infection and Martin lost part of his colon.  Martin now suffers permanent pain as a result of the removal of this part of the colon. The evidence is that it is exceptionally rare for any surgical items to be left inside a person following an operation, no matter how small.  Martin does not blame the surgeons in the original operation but is angry with his doctor and the junior doctor at the hospital, both of whom ignored his symptom. Advise Martin on what basis he might establish a breach of the standard of car owed to him both by his doctor and the junior doctor.

questions.description: Critically discuss the legal meaning of ‘intent’ and the concept of negligence in the tort action of trespass to the per…
questions.text: Critically discuss the legal meaning of ‘intent’ and the concept of negligence in the tort action of trespass to the person.

questions.description: Tim Bottomed Fred is a renowned nightclub in Dublin which attracts rich, young beautiful people.  It is well known throu…
questions.text: Tim Bottomed Fred is a renowned nightclub in Dublin which attracts rich, young beautiful people.  It is well known throughout the country and appears regularly in the media, not only through its own advertising, but also through journalists and social diarists reporting events of the previous night where many of its patrons engage in some hair-raising escapades. One of the reasons for this reporting is a unique concept whereby at one o’clock every night, just as the partygoers are beginning to get tired, the nightclub turns on the special sprinklers, dousing everyone in a heavy mix of vodka and tonic.  The party then gains strength and the DJ calls up the wildest of the clubbers to the stage where he encourages them to undertake weird acts in return for a secret prize. Mort is a nightclub owner in Ballydemagash, about 10 kilometres south of Dublin.  The nightclub has been faced with falling attendance as more people head towards the nightclubs in Dublin city centre. One day Mort has a brainwave.  He decides to re-brand his nightclub and calls it “The Tin Bottomed Fred Experience, brought to you by Mort”.  He uses a similar but different logo to that of Tin Bottomed Fred but is careful to add the word Experience in the same size lettering, although the phrase “brought to you by Mort” is in very small print.  Mort cannot afford to spray the guests with vodka so he substitutes the vodka with blackcurrant juice.  Neither does Mort copy the stage act. The number of people attending Mort’s nightclub grows significantly and he begins to sell T-shirts to the locals with the name of his nightclub printed on them.  Tin Bottomed Fred does not itself sell similar T-shirts. The owners of the Tin Bottomed Fred nightclub seek your advice as to any potential action in tort law against Mort.

questions.description: Jim had a replacement oven door fitted about a year ago, incorporating a new automatic locking system which was designed…
questions.text: Jim had a replacement oven door fitted about a year ago, incorporating a new automatic locking system which was designed as a safety device for children.  Jim bought the replacement door from Belgod Ovens, the manufacturer of the original oven as an approved replacement for his model.  The door was fitted by Darek an approved independent service agent of Belgod Ovens.  Darek followed the instructions provided with the replacement door.  However, the instructions contained a misprint by Belgod Ovens, which resulted in a crucial piece of the locking mechanism being left out.  Darek was surprised to have some pieces left over after the installation but assumed that they had been included in error. Last month Jim’s son and granddaughter, Sarah, came to visit him and, as he was cooking dinner, he engaged the automatic locking device on the oven.  Sarah was playing inside the house while Jim talked to his son on the patio.  Suddenly Jim heard screaming coming from the kitchen and he ran back in to find the oven door open and Sarah covered in hot oil from the roast potatoes that he was cooking.  She was covered in 3rd degree burns from the oil. It has come to light that when Belgod Ovens discovered the error in the instructions, they engaged in a “silent” recall of the affected replacements by contacting everyone they knew who had one of these replacement doors fitted for a so called ‘follow up’ check where they fixed the error without telling the customer.  However, Darek never notified them about Jim’s replacement and had severed his relationship with Belgod Ovens about a month after working for Jim. Advise Sarah as to any statutory right of action she might have, if any, against Belgod Ovens for the injuries that she has suffered.

questions.description: Explain and critically assess the principles governing the defence of consent or waiver under s.34 (1) (b) of the Civil …
questions.text: Explain and critically assess the principles governing the defence of consent or waiver under s.34 (1) (b) of the Civil Liability Act, 1961.

questions.description: John worked for TriState Lift Maintenance.  Last week, John was working on a glass walled lift in an office building.  T…
questions.text: John worked for TriState Lift Maintenance.  Last week, John was working on a glass walled lift in an office building.  The lift had large glass panels, large glass doors and was designed to provide maximum viewing of the central atrium of the building.  However the lift was proving erratic and John was trying to make repairs.  To do this John had to climb on top of the lift and work on the cables by which the lift was suspended.  For this to happen, the lift was stopped mid way between floors. John had arranged to meet his wife Monica for lunch that day.  Monica had arrived early and made her way to the lift.  She could see John through the glass doors of the lift.  She waved and John waved back.  As John was distracted he negligently connected the lift to the power system.  Suddenly the lift began to move upwards and a terrified look came across John’s face.  As the lift moved up Monica clawed desperately at the glass doors but they would not open.  Monica could hear John’s screams as he continued upwards out of Monica’s view.  There was a huge grinding noise and John’s screams stopped along with the lift.  Monica was beside herself.  Suddenly the lift began to move downwards and as it drew level with Monica she could see John clearly relieved and unharmed. Monica now suffers horrific nightmares, clinical depression and constantly replays the whole event over and over in her mind. Advise TriState on any potential tort liability to Monica that might arise in this situation.

questions.description: Explain and critically comment on the main features of the Statute of Limitations (Amendment) Act, 1991.
questions.text: Explain and critically comment on the main features of the Statute of Limitations (Amendment) Act, 1991.

questions.description: Damien owns some land on which there is a derelict property.  In recent times, rumour has swept the local community that…
questions.text: Damien owns some land on which there is a derelict property.  In recent times, rumour has swept the local community that St. Patrick is reputed to appear in the derelict house just before dawn.  Despite the existence of several signs around the boundaries of his land clearly stating ‘PRIVATE PROPERTY’ many locals spend the night outside the derelict property praying and hoping to see St. Patrick.  Some claim to have spoken to St. Patrick who has given advice on how to beat the current recession and financial troubles facing his beloved country. Damien is aware of the fact that people are entering his land without permission but pays no attention to it.  One night, when a small crowd of around 30 people were gathered at the property awaiting the arrival of St Patrick, another group of people arrived.  They were of the view that these events were making a laughing stock of their community and they began to shout abuse at the ‘believers’.  Fearful that they would damage the “shrine of St Patrick”, Luther rushed towards the derelict property and turned to block any attempt at entry by these people.  As Luther spread himself against the wall of the property it collapsed on top of him.  As a result, Luther broke his neck and is now a paraplegic. People began screaming and running when this happened, and in the darkness there was chaos.  Paddy was one of the people who had been shouting abuse.  Scared that he would be blamed for the injury to Luther, he ran but in doing so, tripped over and came down hard on the remnants of an old plough that had been partially buries in the ground.  Paddy punctured his lung. Advise Damien as to his potential tort liability to Paddy and Luther in these circumstances.

questions.description: National Discounters is the anchor tenant in the Ballraha Shopping Centre.  Due to the downturn in the economy, many uni…
questions.text: National Discounters is the anchor tenant in the Ballraha Shopping Centre.  Due to the downturn in the economy, many units were left vacant.  Carrie decided that she would rent a number of these units and reconfigure them so as to create a play area for children.  The aim was to encourage people into the centre so that the increased traffic would result in her renting more units.  In addition to a children’s play gym, there was also a seating area for parents and a small fast food outlet. The children’s playroom was popular beyond Carrie’s wildest expectations.  One day due to carelessness, there is a small fire in the fast food outlet when the grill catches fire.  There is little physical damage to National Discounters, although the Manager notices that some of the paintwork on the wall where the coffee cart is located has blistered from the heat. Unfortunately, the power supply cannot be restored to the shopping centre until it is recertified by the electricity supplier.  As a result National Discounters must close for two of the busiest days of the year when they would expect to make around 30% of their annual sales. Advise Carrie as to the tort principles which would apply in an action by National Discounters for their loss of profits during the two days.

questions.description: Does the law of defamation reflect a satisfactory balance between freedom of expression and the protection of reputation…
questions.text: Does the law of defamation reflect a satisfactory balance between freedom of expression and the protection of reputation?  Give detailed reasons supported by relevant case law or statutory provisions for your answer.

questions.description: Tim is a very jealous husband and Faith is his wife.  Faith has been out at an office party and has had a little too muc…
questions.text: Tim is a very jealous husband and Faith is his wife.  Faith has been out at an office party and has had a little too much to drink.  Gary offered to drive her home.  When they arrived Faith opened the door and invited Gary in for coffee.  As the door closed behind Gary, he spotted Tim sitting in the corner with a rifle in his lap.  Faith asked Tim what he was doing, and Tim replied that he had been up all night just cleaning his gun as he continued polishing the barrel of the rifle. Tim then told Faith that she should go up to bed but that she should lock the front door before going.  Gary said that he should go home.  Tim lifted the rifle and said “I don’t think so!”  Faith laughed, went over and locked the door and headed upstairs.  Tim told Gary to sit down.  Gary sat down.  He told Tim that he would like to go home but Tim asked why he was in such a hurry, that they had lots to talk about.  Gary now stood up and said that he was going.  Tim stood up as well and told Gary that he had had enough of Faith’s behaviour.  Tim moved rapidly towards Gary who stumbled and fell back into the chair.  Tim raised the rifle, pointing it directly at Gary and moved closer so that the rifle was almost touching Gary’s head.  “It is time to send a message to all her fancy men”, said Tim, “say hello to your maker!”  Tim pulled the trigger.  The ‘rifle’ made a clicking sound.  Tim fell around the place laughing.  Gary was crying.  Tim said the rifle was one of his children’s toys.  Gary said he wanted to go and Tim said he was always free to go. As Gary got up, Tim stumbled and fell forward.  The rifle struck Gary in the face and knocked out two teeth. Advise Gary as to any potential claims in tort that he might have against Tim in these circumstances.

questions.description: WarmHouse Ltd is a firm which specialises in home insulation. Rosarie had employed WarmHouse Ltd to install this materia…
questions.text: WarmHouse Ltd is a firm which specialises in home insulation. Rosarie had employed WarmHouse Ltd to install this material in the attic of her home.  Tony, an employee of WarmHouse Ltd, turned up on the agreed date and began the process of installing the insulation. WarmHouse Ltd instructs all its employees that when filling sealed attic spaces the operator has to drill an external entry hole, insert the application hose into the entry hole and switch on the machine.  WarmHouse Ltd trains their operators that it is permissible to set the machine on automatic but that the relevant pressure gauge has to be monitored at least once every five minutes.  If the pressure drops this is indicative that the operation is complete and the machine must be turned off immediately. Tony set up the machine to fill the closed attic of Rosarie’s house.  As the procedure began, Rosarie received a call from her son’s school saying that he was quite sick and that she needed to collect him.  Unfortunately the WarmHouse insulation machine was blocking Rosarie’s car.  Rosarie was frantic and begged Tony to drive her to the school.  Tony was unsure, but Rosarie was insistent that it wouldn’t take 5 minutes.  Reluctantly, Tony agreed and drove her to the school in his WarmHouse Ltd van.  On the way back, Tony was involved in a small accident with another car.  No one was hurt but this delayed them by around 20 minutes while they exchanged insurance details. When Tony returned, he found that the machine should have been turned off a long time ago and since it was not, too much insulation was pumped in, causing the internal ceiling of Rosarie’s house to collapse causing significant damages. Draft a memorandum to WarmHouse Ltd outlining their potential liability in tort with respect to the damage to Rosarie’s home.

questions.description: Brian, an accountant, is engaged BY Kown plc to prepare a financial report for the purpose of discussions with Tiger Ltd…
questions.text: Brian, an accountant, is engaged BY Kown plc to prepare a financial report for the purpose of discussions with Tiger Ltd concerning the sale of securitised mortgage investment products with a high rate of return.  In preparing the report Brian relied exclusively on documentation and information supplied by the directors of Kown plc as he was not personally familiar with the investment product in question. During the course of the negotiations with Kown plc and Tiger Ltd, Seamus, the majority shareholder of Tiger Ltd, asked Brian if they could meet privately to discuss some concerns that Seamus had.  At that meeting, Seamus asked Brian if it were his money would he make the investment.  Brian thought about it for a bit and then said that from the information he had to hand, these securities represented outstanding potential for investors with a low risk element.  Seamus said that was helpful in finalising his decision. Subsequently, Tiger Ltd agreed to buy €100 million of these investments.  Within six weeks the value of these investments had doubled to €200 million.  At that point, Brian personally investigated the investment and realised that they were a significantly high risk product but given how much the investments had risen decided not to contact Seamus.  When the credit crunch struck four weeks later, the value of the investments fell back to €95 million.  Given Tiger Ltd’s exposure to these investments, a well respected credit rating agency downgraded Tiger Ltd’s credit rating.  This meant that Tiger Ltd was unable to re-finance its loans at affordable rates of interest.  Tiger Ltd defaulted on its debts and was put into liquidation. Advise Brian as to his potential liability for the losses suffered by Tiger Ltd and Seamus.

questions.text: Critically appraise the legal rules governing an employer’s liability for torts committed by its employees.

questions.description: Jack Airs is an opinion columnist for The Sunday View newspaper.  Last week Jack submitted an article for publication to…
questions.text: Jack Airs is an opinion columnist for The Sunday View newspaper.  Last week Jack submitted an article for publication to the editor of the newspaper about John Dormer, a leading politician.  The proposed article contained the following paragraph. Dormer is a politician with little done and less to do.  That won’t stop him from clawing his way into a position of power well above that to which he is entitled.  But incompetence is the least of Dormer’s crimes.  I have it on good authority that 	Dormer has for years pushed legislation that has benefited companies in which he 	has a financial interest.  I believe that a survey of his bank account over the last ten 	years appear more suited to that of a drug dealer than an honest politician. As for his family life, I know for a fact that there is no point in asking Dormer’s wife 	where he spends his nights in Dublin.  Better to ask well known homosexual 	restaurant owner Ricardo.  John Dormer is unfit for public office. The paragraph concludes with Jack Airs’ standard sign off line that he uses at the end of every one of his articles: This is just one man’s opinion. The editor of the Sunday View was concerned about the piece and sent a draft copy to the deader of Dormer’s political party and Dormer’s wife for their comments before deciding whether or not to publish the item.  In the meantime, Jack Air included the paragraph on his personal blog, which is hosted on the Sunday View newspaper website. Advise the Sunday View if they have any defences to a defamation action by Dormer against them.

questions.description: Critically analyse the approach currently taken by the Irish Courts in respect of negligently inflicted psychiatric inju…
questions.text: Critically analyse the approach currently taken by the Irish Courts in respect of negligently inflicted psychiatric injury.

questions.description: Steven owns a public bar in a busy Dublin neighbourhood mixed use development.  Together with shops and offices, there a…
questions.text: Steven owns a public bar in a busy Dublin neighbourhood mixed use development.  Together with shops and offices, there are several residential apartments.  Claire purchased a luxury apartment in the development which was located to the rear of the bar.  When Claire purchased the apartment, Steven’s bar was a relatively quiet bar with a small but steady elderly clientele. Unfortunately, Steven passed away about six months ago and his son Trapper took over the running of the pub.  Conscious of the upcoming Olympics, Trapper decided to renovate the bar by turning it into a sports bar.  From a commercial point of view this was a great success.  Although Trapper lost his existing clientele, he acquired a large news sports oriented clientele. This changed the nature of the bar entirely.  During opening hours the bar was now exceptionally noisy with the clientele roaring both their approval and dissatisfaction with various sporting events.  At closing time, the clientele would continue their arguments over these events onto the public seating outside the bar, making even further noise until well into the night. As the Olympics drew to a close, Claire had expected that the bar would quieten but this was not the case.  Instead the bar became an even greater focus for the sports oriented clientele.  Claire has complained to Trapper who says that there is not much he can do during the time when the bar is open but that he will employ a private security firm to move patrons on after closing time. Advise Claire on any causes of action in tort which may be available to her.

questions.description: Due to his employer’s negligence Bertie fell while at work, leading to a slipped disc in his back.  Bertie was in excruc…
questions.text: Due to his employer’s negligence Bertie fell while at work, leading to a slipped disc in his back.  Bertie was in excruciating pain.  The doctor treating him, Mary, indicated that the only option was for Bertie to have an operation to remove the disc but there were risks as there were with all operations.  However, without the operation Bertie would have excruciating pain for the rest of his life.  Mary did not think it important to make clear to Bertie that there was a statistical 0.5 to 1.0% chance that the operation might result in Bertie never being able to walk again, given that Mary had personally performed many identical operations without any difficulties. Unfortunately, despite the fact that the treatment was carried out with due care, Bertie was unable to walk again.  Barite became very depressed and as a result, Bertie’s wife left him, taking their children with her.  The divorce proceedings were extremely bitter and Bertie became addicted to anti-depressant drugs prescribed by his GP.  Bertie then took up gambling and lots all his money and currently lives in public housing. Mary admits that she was negligent in not telling barite about the risk, but argues that this information would not have altered the fact that Bertie had to have the operation in question.  Bertie’s employer claims that although they were negligent with respect to the original injury, they are not liable for the consequences of Bertie’s operation as Mary was responsible for this.  Both Mary and Bertie’s employer claim that, even if they are liable to some degree, they cannot be liable for Bertie’s marriage breakdown which is the primary cause of his current situation. Advise Bertie as to the principles applicable to determining the extent of the liability of Mary and his employers.

questions.description: X-On produce an infra red heat massager, the XR1000, designed to alleviate deep seated muscle pain.  The XR1000 is a pop…
questions.text: X-On produce an infra red heat massager, the XR1000, designed to alleviate deep seated muscle pain.  The XR1000 is a popular model which sells in large numbers to the general public.  The basic principle behind the XR1000 is the use of a certain infra red light that when applied close to the skin, provides a gentle warming sensation deep within the body.  The unit was designed in Ireland but is manufactured under licence in China. X-On undertake random quality testing of the XR1000 when they arrive in Ireland before distributing them to retailers.  Due to exceptional demand coming up to Christmas, a significant number are released to retailers without this quality testing taking place.  X-On nonetheless continue retrospectively quality testing of the released units.  During this testing it comes to light that one batch of the product is faulty in that the infra red light is far too strong and is likely to cause burning of the skin.  X-On issues a recall to all its retailers in the form of a flyer which contains all the relevant details of the XR1000s involved but does not show a picture of the product. Anna is from Poland but living in Ireland.  She purchased one of the defective XR1000 models.  She cannot read English and there are no Polish language instructions with the XR1000.  On Christmas Eve, Anna had severe pain in her eyes following a long day at her job.  She applied the XR1000 to her eyes for around 30 seconds.  When she opened her eyes she found she was permanently blind.  Although the instructions clearly state that the product should not be used on eyes, her blindness arises only because of the defective nature of the infra red system in the recalled models. Advise X-On as to any potential liability in tort to Anna.

P. 384) Critically discuss tort liability in respect of animals.

questions.description: Fred was taking an early Sunday morning stroll on the beach when he came across a dead whale which had been washed up on…
questions.text: Fred was taking an early Sunday morning stroll on the beach when he came across a dead whale which had been washed up on the shoreline.  It was a huge Blue Whale.  These whales are not normally found in Irish waters and Fred could not believe his eyes.  Fred called Henry, the local owner of the beach. Henry was not prepared for this sort of event.  Around three days later, people began to notice a smell from the whale and Henry realised that it was decomposing.  The whale was too big to be buried and the coastline was too shallow for a tugboat to get close enough to pull the whale back out to sea.  Henry decided that the whale would have to be broken into smaller pieces and each piece then trucked away.  However, given the size of the dead whale, he realised this would take many weeks and that the smell would soon be fairly noxious.  Accordingly he decided that he would use a small amount of explosives to quickly break the whale into smaller more manageable pieces. The event was set for 9am on Tuesday morning and had generated quite a buzz in the local community.  About 100 people turned up to watch the spectacle and Henry had set up a cordon keeping people around 15 metres away from the whale.  At about 8.55am, Henry’s wife handed him an email from the US Coast Guard.  It said that they had heard of his plans on the internet but from bitter experience they could not recommend this course of action.  They asked him to cancel the event and contact them as soon as possible.  Henry was having none of this.  He ordered the event to proceed.  At 9am, the explosives were detonated.  However, too much explosives were used and the pieces of whale shot high into the sky and fell down to earth in a 30 metre radius.  One piece of the whale fell on Fred as he was fleeing the scene and broke his leg. Advise Fred if he has any cause of action against Henry in these circumstances.

questions.description: Debra had driven Robert to the airport as he was flying on Emerald Airlines to the United States for work.  Debra kissed…
questions.text: Debra had driven Robert to the airport as he was flying on Emerald Airlines to the United States for work.  Debra kissed Robert goodbye at the security gate, went back to the car and began to drive home.  As she left the airport, she thought she heard a loud explosion, but she paid no attention to it. She was about half way home when there was an emergency news item on Newsbabble 63FM.  Apparently an Emerald Airlines plane preparing to take-off to the United States had encountered trouble and that anyone who had relatives or friends on that flight should contact Emerald Airlines directly as soon as possible.  Debra immediately pulled off the road and frantically dialled the number of Emerald Airlines on her mobile phone.  It was engaged.  Debra became more and more frantic.  She kept trying to contact Emerald Airlines but kept getting a busy signal. In violation of a news embargo, the DJ on Newsbabble 63FM, said that there had been a terrible tragedy at the airport, that an Emerald Airlines flight to the United States had caught fire on take-off and had exploded, killing all on board.  Debra threw her mobile out the window of the car, and sobbing uncontrollably, Debra gathered herself together sufficiently to drive home. As she opened the door of her home, she saw what she thought was Robert’s ghost waiting for her.  She passed out cold.  In fact Robert had been turned back at U.S. immigration since his passport was out of date and had never boarded the plane. However, Debra now spends most nights having nightmares, believing that Robert is dead and that she is sleeping with his decomposing body next to her. Advise Debra if she has any potential cause of action under Irish tort law.

questions.description: Bling Clothes is a small retail fashion shop selling ladies clothes.  They were looking for an advertisement gimmick to …
questions.text: Bling Clothes is a small retail fashion shop selling ladies clothes.  They were looking for an advertisement gimmick to promote their store.  Gerry, the owner of Bling Clothes could not believe his luck when Kate Stone, a famous international model, came into the store, dressed in torn sweat pants, a streaked fleece and hair dishevelled.  As Kate was wandering around the store, Gerry hid behind a mirror and began taking a couple of pictures of Kate as she was browsing through the racks of dresses. Gerry then put the camera away and came out to Kate, and began advising her about what would look best on her.  He offered to dress her from head to toe and picked out an ensemble for her to wear.  Kate was dubious but tried on the items.  When she came out of the dressing room, Gerry again took pictures of her, again without Kate being aware of it. Kate was very dismissive of the choices made by Gerry, saying that she would never work again if she were seen dressed in this outfit.  Kate changed back into her own clothes and flounced out of the shop. About a week later, a full page ad in the largest national newspaper showed two pictures: a before and after of Kate Stone.  The ‘before’ picture showed Kate in her unkempt state and the ‘after’ picture showed Kate wearing the clothes picked by Gerry.  Under them the headline ran: “Kate Stone – choosing her own style at Bling Clothes.”  Kate believes that it will be very difficult for her to be taken seriously in her career as a model if people believe that these clothes represented her own style. Advise Kate if she has any cause of action against Bling Clothing under Irish tort law.

questions.description: Herman was employed as a delivery driver by FoDoX Ltd.  Recently the manager of the distribution centre discovered that …
questions.text: Herman was employed as a delivery driver by FoDoX Ltd.  Recently the manager of the distribution centre discovered that a small, but valuable, parcel of goods was missing.  It was 6pm and the workers had finished their shift but the manager insisted that all employees would have to be searched and questioned before they could leave. Herman refused to be searched and said that he had to leave to pick up his child from the crèche and that he could not afford to be late. Herman insisted that the matter could be resolved the next day and that the item was probably simply misplaced.  Samson, a burly security guard employed by FoDoX Ltd became quite aggressive to Herman, and stood so as to prevent Herman going through the exit doorway. Samson said to Herman “You can try to leave but bear in mind, I have killed before and I am willing to do so again.  It’s up to you!”  Herman fearing for his life began to walk away when the manager ordered Samson to seize Herman’s bag and search for the missing parcel. Samson yanked the bag from Herman’s hand.  Herman turned to strike Samson, but Samson simply ducked and then knocked Herman to the ground. Samson opened the bag but there was nothing inside other than the remains of a half eaten lunch and an old newspaper. Herman ran to the door but it was locked.  Samson ran after him, pinning him against the doorway. The manager told Herman that he would have to consent to be personally searched if he wanted to leave. Herman reluctantly agreed. When nothing was found, Herman was allowed leave. Advise Herman as to any rights in tort which he may have against FoDoX Ltd.

questions.description: Vanguard Motors manufactures the popular VM 100 family saloon, which has, since its introduction 18 months ago, held the…
questions.text: Vanguard Motors manufactures the popular VM 100 family saloon, which has, since its introduction 18 months ago, held the number one sales slot.  John bought a VM 100 about three months ago.  He was driving along the motorway at around 120km/hour when the vehicle suddenly lost power without warning.  John managed to steer the vehicle safely into the hard shoulder where he called for roadside assistance. When the vehicle was inspected by Vanguard Motors, they informed John that it was a one off technical hitch whereby an electrical lead had failed for no apparent reason.  They replaced the lead and the car returned to normal operation. Subsequently, Vanguard Motors discovered that the design of the VM 100 was defective.  Under very rare conditions, the lead could fail due to a short circuit.  The defect could be remedied by using a higher rated lead, but it would take about 6 months before there was sufficient supply of these higher rated leads to replace them in all of the VM 100 which had been sold.  As there was little likelihood of any damage or injury arising from the failure of the lead, Vanguard Motors decided not to make a recall announcement until they had sufficient stock of these leads to change all the affected vehicles. John was again driving on the motorway when his VM 100 suddenly lost power.  This time however the road was icy and, without the anti-lock braking system, the car spun across two lanes and into the dividing barrier.  John was rendered a paraplegic. Advise Vanguard Motors as to their potential liability in tort.
